Pott’s puffy tumor – frontal sinusitis +
osteomyelitis

Adamantinomatous
craniopharyngioma-wet keratin, palisading epithelium,
stellate reticulum

Pituitary adenoma: Acidophiles
(40%: PRL, GH, FSH/LH), basophils (10%: ACTH, TSH), null cells (50%)

Rathke’s cleft cyst - watery
mucous fluid w columnar/cuboid epithelium
Dura extending between anterior clinoid and planum sphenoidale, covering the optic nerve

A large olfactory groove meningioma with decreased olfaction, ipsilateral optic nerve atrophy, and contralateral papilledema. Contralateral papilledema is from increased ICP, while ipsilateral atrophy is from direct mass effect.

front: Describe the classic findings in Foster-Kennedy syndrome
back: olfactory groove meningioma, ipsilateral anosmia, ipsilateral optic atrophy, contralateral papilledema
